summary: A single-level measurement of visceral adipose tissue cannot adequately represent total visceral fat volume because accurate quantification relies on volumetric imaging techniques involving integrated data across multiple anatomical planes and complex computational modeling. Even when performed at the optimal L4–L5 level, such assessments require advanced digital reconstructions to derive true volume—making them fundamentally different from simplified area-based measurements. \boxed{B}

Even when performed at the optimal L4–L5 level, such assessments require advanced digital reconstructions to derive true volume—making them fundamentally different from simplified area-based measurements. \boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
